name: Mexican Vegetable Soup
recipeInstructions:
1. Brown the ground beef in a 5-quart Dutch oven.
2. Drain excess fat from the beef.
3. Add taco seasoning mix, tomato juice, chili beans, frozen vegetables, and tomato paste to the pot.
4. Stir well to combine all ingredients.
5. Bring the mixture to a boil over high heat.
6. Reduce the heat to medium-low and simmer uncovered for 20-25 minutes or until the vegetables are tender, stirring occasionally.
7. Serve each bowl topped with corn chips and shredded cheddar cheese.
Makes 11 servings of approximately 1 cup each.
Nutritional Information:
Per serving: 340 calories, 18 g protein, 22 g carbohydrate, 17 g fat, 50 mg cholesterol, 1180 mg sodium
Source: Pillsbury Classic Cookbook, #86, p. 13 (33rd Bake-Off)
Submitted by Nancy Hindernach, Dearborn Heights, MI
